message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending tasks, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

'./.claude/progress.txt' is:
- your persistent memory.
- Important errors must be registered in order to avoid expending tokens again.
- Never run tasks not registered in it.
- Register new tasks if needed, respecting the current structure.


WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project Facts from CLAUDE.md
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   a. Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   b. Execute task (respecting current project structure and ./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
   c. Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. IF THE COMMIT FAILS (pre-commit hook / lint error):
      - Add a [P0] priority task to progress.txt describing the exact errors
      - Retry the commit with `git commit --no-verify` to save your work
      - The NEXT iteration will fix the lint errors as its top priority
   i. Output status block (see below)
   j. Stop

- Nenhuma tarefa deve ser considerada completa sem testar com Playwright MVP 
  e tirar screenshots para visualização e visualizar.
  
OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
7. Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
8. Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)

   970→# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
   971→- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
   972→  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts — evaluateRule() function with:
   973→  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
   974→  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
   975→  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
   976→  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
   977→  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
   978→  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
   979→  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
   980→  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
   981→  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
   982→  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
   983→  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

   985→Context:

   987→  and, or, ! (logical)

   989→  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)

   991→- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
   992→- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
   993→  retornam false")
   994→- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
   995→  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
   996→- Must be pure function, no side effects
   997→- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/
   998→
   999→Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
  1000→comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts — evaluateRule() function with:
  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

---

## SDUI Adapter Patterns & Models — Comprehensive Summary

### Overview
The SDUI (Server-Driven UI) system is a JSON-based component rendering architecture that enables backends to define screen layouts without requiring frontend code changes. The system uses:
- **Type system** (`SDUIScreen`, `SDUIComponent`) for type-safe JSON serialization
- **Adapters** (component renderers) that map SDUI JSON to React Native Design System components
- **Registry** for component registration and lookup
- **Utilities** for interpolation (`{{var}}` syntax), JSON Logic evaluation, and validation
- **Mocks** for Storybook demos and testing

---

## 1. SDUI Type System (`src/services/sdui/models/index.ts`)

### Key Exports

| Type/Interface | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `SDUIScreen` | Root screen structure with `id`, `context`, `header?`, `body[]`, `footer?` |
| `SDUIComponent<T>` | Component definition: `type`, `props`, `visible?` |
| `SDUIComponentProps` | Type-safe props: strings, numbers, booleans, nulls, conditionals, rich text, validation rules, or nested objects/arrays |
| `SDUIContext` | Runtime data object accessible via `{{var}}` interpolation in props |
| `SDUIConditional` | Boolean rule-based visibility/enablement: `{ rule: SDUIRule }` |
| `SDUIValidation` | Input validation with rules and error messages |
| `SDUIRichText` | Inline formatted text with nodes: `text`, `link`, `icon`, `br` |
| `SDUIRule` | JSON Logic subset: comparison (`==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) and logical (`and`, `or`, `!`) operators |
| `SDUIVarOperator` | Dot-notation context access: `{ var: 'balance.pointsFormatted' }` |

### Known Component Types (Registry)

```typescript
const SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES = [
  'heading', 'text', 'balance_display', 'points_input', 'button', 'link', 'alert',
  'list_text_icon', 'separator', 'icon_text_row', 'section_heading', 'nav_header',
  'rich_text', 'voucher_card', 'empty_state',
  // Extract/Transaction screen components:
  'points_card', 'tab_bar', 'transaction_item', 'date_separator',
];
```

---

## 2. Adapter Pattern (`src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts` + individual adapters)

### Registration Pattern

```typescript
// adapters/index.ts
const adapters: Record<string, SDUIComponentRenderer> = {
  heading: HeadingAdapter as SDUIComponentRenderer,
  text: TextAdapter as SDUIComponentRenderer,
  points_card: PointsCardAdapter as SDUIComponentRenderer,
  // ... all adapters registered here
};

export function registerAllAdapters(): void {
  Object.entries(adapters).forEach(([type, renderer]) => {
    registerToDefault(type, renderer);
  });
}
```

### Adapter Interface (Signature)

Every adapter follows this pattern:

```typescript
export default function ComponentNameAdapter({
  props,      // Typed props extending SDUIComponentProps
  context,    // SDUIContext from screen (for interpolation)
}: {
  props: ComponentNameProps;
  context: SDUIContext;
}) {
  return <DesignSystemComponent {...mappedProps} />;
}
```

### Examples

#### Simple Adapter: HeadingAdapter
```typescript
interface HeadingProps extends SDUIComponentProps {
  text: string;
  color?: string;
  mt?: string;
  mb?: string;
  align?: string;
  testID?: string;
}

export default function HeadingAdapter({ props }) {
  const { text, color = 'INTENSE_COLOR_05', mt, mb, align, testID } = props;
  return (
    <Text type="headingMDL" color={color} mt={mt} mb={mb} align={align} testID={testID}>
      {text}
    </Text>
  );
}
```

#### Complex Adapter: TransactionItemAdapter
- Maps semantic colors (`green`, `red`, `gray`, `orange`) to design system colors
- Handles conditional icon display via `showIcon` prop
- Includes icon circle, two-line text (title + typeLabel), and value alignment

```typescript
interface TransactionItemProps extends SDUIComponentProps {
  icon: string;
  title: string;
  value: string;
  valueColor?: string;           // 'green' | 'red' | 'gray' | 'orange'
  typeLabel?: string;
  showIcon?: boolean;            // Conditional rendering
  mb?: string;
}

const VALUE_COLOR_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  green: 'ACCEPT_COLOR_01',
  red: 'DENY_COLOR_01',
  gray: 'INTENSE_COLOR_03',
  orange: 'ALERT_COLOR_01',
};
```

Layout: `[icon circle] [title/typeLabel] [value →]` with `minHeight: 56`

#### Tab Bar Adapter
- **State management**: Uses internal `useState` for active tab
- **Props**:
  - `tabs`: Array of `{ id: number; title: string }`
  - `mt`, `mb`, `ml`: Spacing
- **Render**: Row of pressable tab buttons with bottom border indicator (pink `#E91E63` for active)

#### Date Separator Adapter
- **Minimal**: Single text label with optional spacing
- **Props**: `label`, `mt`, `mb` (defaults: `mt='xs'`, `mb='xxxs'`)
- **Example usage**: "20 de fevereiro" between transaction groups

#### Points Card Adapter
- Wraps `CardPoints` design component
- **Props**: `icon`, `title`, `valuePoints`, `iconInfo?`, `hideBottom?`, spacing
- **Default spacing**: `ml/mr='smaller'`, `mb='xxxs'`
- **Always sets**: `isLoading={false}`, `onPressIconInfo={() => {}}`

#### Voucher Card Adapter
- **High-complexity**: Maps extensive data to `CardVehicleTags` (version 2.0)
- **Tag mapping**: `{ success, error, alert, highlight }` → semantic colors
- **Props**: icon, vehicleName, title/titleDescription, date/dateNumber, statusTitle/status, typeTag, ghostButtonText
- **Always sets**: `type='extended'`, `version='2.0'`, stub button handler

---

## 3. Screen Renderer (`src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx`)

### Rendering Pipeline

```
Screen (JSON) → ScreenRenderer
  ├── isVisible(component, context) → evaluateRule() → boolean
  ├── separateConditionals(props) → { interpolatable, conditionals }
  ├── interpolateDeep(interpolatable, context) → resolved values
  └── getRenderer(type) → SDUIComponentRenderer
      └── <Renderer props={resolvedProps} context={context} />
```

### Key Steps

1. **Visibility Check**: `component.visible?.rule` evaluated against context
2. **Conditional Separation**: Props with `{ rule: ... }` are kept as-is (resolved by adapter or re-evaluated)
3. **Interpolation**: `{{context.path.to.value}}` in string/number props → actual values
4. **Registry Lookup**: Type → component renderer function
5. **Fallback**: Missing adapters render yellow warning with type/props

### Layout
```jsx
<View flex={1}>
  {header && renderComponent(header)}
  <View flex={1}>
    {body.map((component, idx) => renderComponent(component))}
  </View>
  {footer && renderComponent(footer)}
</View>
```

---

## 4. Mock Payloads

### Pattern: Mocks Structure
Located in `src/services/sdui/mocks/`:
- **File per screen**: `voucherList.ts`, `extractHistory.ts`, `voucherForm.ts`, etc.
- **Export variants**: `...Loaded`, `...Empty`, `...Filtered` for different states
- **Type**: `SDUIScreen`

### Example: Extract History (`extractHistory.ts`)

#### Context
```typescript
context: {
  balance: { pointsFormatted: '15.320' },
  transactions: [
    {
      icon: 'icon-tag',
      title: 'Pedágio Rodovia Anhanguera',
      value: '+ 150 pontos',
      valueColor: 'green',
      typeLabel: 'Ganhos',
    },
    // ... 7 more
  ],
}
```

#### Body Components (in order)
```
1. nav_header (title: 'Extrato')
2. points_card (balance display with interpolation)
3. section_heading (label: 'Lançamentos')
4. separator (horizontal line)
5. tab_bar (tabs: ['Todos', 'A expirar'])
6. date_separator (label: '20 de fevereiro')
7–8. transaction_item × 2 (interpolated from transactions[0–1])
9. date_separator (label: '18 de fevereiro')
10–11. transaction_item × 2 (interpolated from transactions[2–3])
... [pattern repeats for other dates]
```

#### Interpolation Examples
```json
{
  "type": "points_card",
  "props": {
    "valuePoints": "{{balance.pointsFormatted}}"  // Resolves to "15.320"
  }
},
{
  "type": "transaction_item",
  "props": {
    "icon": "{{transactions.0.icon}}",            // Resolves to "icon-tag"
    "value": "{{transactions.0.value}}",          // Resolves to "+ 150 pontos"
    "valueColor": "{{transactions.0.valueColor}}"  // Resolves to "green"
  }
}
```

#### Empty State
```typescript
extractHistoryEmpty: {
  context: { balance: { pointsFormatted: '500' } },
  body: [
    points_card, section_heading, separator, tab_bar,
    empty_state(icon: 'icon-invoice-error', title: 'Para ganhar pontos...')
  ]
}
```

---

## 5. Story Pattern (`src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx`, `SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`)

### Structure

```typescript
import ScreenRenderer from '../services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer';
import { registerAllAdapters } from '../services/sdui/adapters';
import { useSDUIJsonEditor } from '../../.storybook/addons/json-editor';
import { voucherListLoaded, voucherListEmpty } from '../services/sdui/mocks';

registerAllAdapters();  // One-time setup

export default function Demo() {
  const [view, setView] = useState<'list' | 'empty'>('list');
  const screen = useSDUIJsonEditor(SCREENS[view]);  // Edit JSON live
  
  return (
    <ScreenRenderer screen={screen} />
  );
}
```

### Two Story Patterns

1. **Simple Demo**: Single mode with toggle between states
   - ViewToggle component for 'list' | 'empty' | 'filtered'
   - JSON editor addon allows live editing of SDUI payload
   - Real-time rendering

2. **Side-by-Side Comparison**:
   - **Left**: Traditional React components hardcoded data
   - **Right**: Same visual via SDUI JSON + ScreenRenderer
   - Demonstrates that JSON-driven approach achieves identical UI
   - Shows decoupling benefit: backend can change content without deploy

### Extract Story Example
- `Demo`: Load/Empty toggle with JSON editor
- `Comparison`: Real screen (hardcoded) vs SDUI (JSON)
  - Real uses `CardPoints`, `Icon`, nested `Box`, manual date grouping
  - SDUI uses context data + adapters, backend controls layout

---

## 6. Existing Adapters Summary Table

| Adapter | Design Component | Props Style | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| HeadingAdapter | Text (headingMDL) | text, color, spacing | Simple mapping |
| TextAdapter | Text | text, type, color, spacing | Flexible typography |
| PointsCardAdapter | CardPoints | icon, title, valuePoints | Spacing defaults: ml/mr='smaller' |
| TabBarAdapter | Custom (Pressable + Text + Separator) | tabs, spacing | Internal state for active tab |
| TransactionItemAdapter | Custom (Box + Icon + Text) | icon, title, value, valueColor, typeLabel, showIcon | Color map: green→ACCEPT, red→DENY, etc. |
| DateSeparatorAdapter | Box + Text | label, spacing | Minimal: just a date label |
| VoucherCardAdapter | CardVehicleTags | icon, vehicleName, title, titleDescription, date, dateNumber, statusTitle, status, typeTag | Tag mapping; extensive props |
| BalanceDisplayAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| ButtonAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| LinkAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| AlertAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| ListTextIconAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| SeparatorAdapter | SeparatorHorizontal | bgColor, height, width, spacing | Horizontal line utility |
| IconTextRowAdapter | Box + Icon + Text | icon, iconColor, iconSize, text, textType, textColor, spacing | Flex row layout; reusable pattern |
| SectionHeadingAdapter | (inferred: Text) | text, spacing | Likely section labels |
| EmptyStateAdapter | Custom (Box + Icon + Box + Text) | icon, iconColor, bgColor, title, description | Centered card with rounded icon circle (80×80) |
| RichTextAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| PointsInputAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |

---

## 7. Registry & Resolution (`src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts`)

### API
```typescript
// Create isolated registry
const registry = createRegistry();

// Register component
registerComponent(registry, 'my_type', MyAdapter);

// Retrieve for rendering
const Renderer = getRenderer(registry, 'my_type');

// Default registry (module singleton)
registerToDefault('heading', HeadingAdapter);
const defaultRegistry = getDefaultRegistry();
```

### Lookup in ScreenRenderer
```typescript
const Renderer = getRenderer(registry, component.type);
if (!Renderer) return <FallbackComponent type={...} props={...} />;
return <Renderer props={interpolatedProps} context={context} />;
```

---

## 8. Key Props/Spacing Values (Design System)

### Spacing Tokens
Common in SDUI props:
- `mt`, `mb`, `ml`, `mr`, `pd` (padding), `br` (border radius)
- Values: `'xxs'`, `'xs'`, `'xxxs'`, `'nano'`, `'smaller'`, `'small'`, `'medium'`, `'large'`, etc.

### Colors (Design System Palette)
- `INTENSE_COLOR_01` → `INTENSE_COLOR_05` (intensity scale)
- `ACCEPT_COLOR_01` (green)
- `DENY_COLOR_01` (red)
- `ALERT_COLOR_01` (orange)
- `BRAND_BASE_01`, `BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02`, `BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03`
- `LIGHT_BASE_01`, `LIGHT_BASE_02`

### Typography
- `type`: `'headingMDL'`, `'bodyMD'`, `'paragraphMD'`, `'paragraphXXXL'`, `'subtitleXXL'`, `'captionMD'`, `'captionSM'`, etc.

---

## 9. Mocks Export Pattern (`src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts`)

```typescript
export { voucherListLoaded, voucherListEmpty, voucherListFiltered } from './voucherList';
export { extractHistoryLoaded, extractHistoryEmpty } from './extractHistory';
export { voucherFormAbastecimento, voucherFormEstacionamento } from './voucherForm';
// ... etc
```

All mocks are `SDUIScreen` objects that can be rendered directly via `ScreenRenderer`.

---

## 10. Practical Usage Summary

### To Create a New Screen Mock:
1. Define context object with all data needed
2. Create body array with components referencing context via `{{path.to.data}}`

---

## SDUI Adapter Patterns & Models — Comprehensive Summary

### Overview
The SDUI (Server-Driven UI) system is a JSON-based component rendering architecture that enables backends to define screen layouts without requiring frontend code changes. The system uses:
- **Type system** (`SDUIScreen`, `SDUIComponent`) for type-safe JSON serialization
- **Adapters** (component renderers) that map SDUI JSON to React Native Design System components
- **Registry** for component registration and lookup
- **Utilities** for interpolation (`{{var}}` syntax), JSON Logic evaluation, and validation
- **Mocks** for Storybook demos and testing

---

## 1. SDUI Type System (`src/services/sdui/models/index.ts`)

### Key Exports

| Type/Interface | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `SDUIScreen` | Root screen structure with `id`, `context`, `header?`, `body[]`, `footer?` |
| `SDUIComponent<T>` | Component definition: `type`, `props`, `visible?` |
| `SDUIComponentProps` | Type-safe props: strings, numbers, booleans, nulls, conditionals, rich text, validation rules, or nested objects/arrays |
| `SDUIContext` | Runtime data object accessible via `{{var}}` interpolation in props |
| `SDUIConditional` | Boolean rule-based visibility/enablement: `{ rule: SDUIRule }` |
| `SDUIValidation` | Input validation with rules and error messages |
| `SDUIRichText` | Inline formatted text with nodes: `text`, `link`, `icon`, `br` |
| `SDUIRule` | JSON Logic subset: comparison (`==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) and logical (`and`, `or`, `!`) operators |
| `SDUIVarOperator` | Dot-notation context access: `{ var: 'balance.pointsFormatted' }` |

### Known Component Types (Registry)

```typescript
const SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES = [
  'heading', 'text', 'balance_display', 'points_input', 'button', 'link', 'alert',
  'list_text_icon', 'separator', 'icon_text_row', 'section_heading', 'nav_header',
  'rich_text', 'voucher_card', 'empty_state',
  // Extract/Transaction screen components:
  'points_card', 'tab_bar', 'transaction_item', 'date_separator',
];
```

---

## 2. Adapter Pattern (`src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts` + individual adapters)

### Registration Pattern

```typescript
// adapters/index.ts
const adapters: Record<string, SDUIComponentRenderer> = {
  heading: HeadingAdapter as SDUIComponentRenderer,
  text: TextAdapter as SDUIComponentRenderer,
  points_card: PointsCardAdapter as SDUIComponentRenderer,
  // ... all adapters registered here
};

export function registerAllAdapters(): void {
  Object.entries(adapters).forEach(([type, renderer]) => {
    registerToDefault(type, renderer);
  });
}
```

### Adapter Interface (Signature)

Every adapter follows this pattern:

```typescript
export default function ComponentNameAdapter({
  props,      // Typed props extending SDUIComponentProps
  context,    // SDUIContext from screen (for interpolation)
}: {
  props: ComponentNameProps;
  context: SDUIContext;
}) {
  return <DesignSystemComponent {...mappedProps} />;
}
```

### Examples

#### Simple Adapter: HeadingAdapter
```typescript
interface HeadingProps extends SDUIComponentProps {
  text: string;
  color?: string;
  mt?: string;
  mb?: string;
  align?: string;
  testID?: string;
}

export default function HeadingAdapter({ props }) {
  const { text, color = 'INTENSE_COLOR_05', mt, mb, align, testID } = props;
  return (
    <Text type="headingMDL" color={color} mt={mt} mb={mb} align={align} testID={testID}>
      {text}
    </Text>
  );
}
```

#### Complex Adapter: TransactionItemAdapter
- Maps semantic colors (`green`, `red`, `gray`, `orange`) to design system colors
- Handles conditional icon display via `showIcon` prop
- Includes icon circle, two-line text (title + typeLabel), and value alignment

```typescript
interface TransactionItemProps extends SDUIComponentProps {
  icon: string;
  title: string;
  value: string;
  valueColor?: string;           // 'green' | 'red' | 'gray' | 'orange'
  typeLabel?: string;
  showIcon?: boolean;            // Conditional rendering
  mb?: string;
}

const VALUE_COLOR_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  green: 'ACCEPT_COLOR_01',
  red: 'DENY_COLOR_01',
  gray: 'INTENSE_COLOR_03',
  orange: 'ALERT_COLOR_01',
};
```

Layout: `[icon circle] [title/typeLabel] [value →]` with `minHeight: 56`

#### Tab Bar Adapter
- **State management**: Uses internal `useState` for active tab
- **Props**:
  - `tabs`: Array of `{ id: number; title: string }`
  - `mt`, `mb`, `ml`: Spacing
- **Render**: Row of pressable tab buttons with bottom border indicator (pink `#E91E63` for active)

#### Date Separator Adapter
- **Minimal**: Single text label with optional spacing
- **Props**: `label`, `mt`, `mb` (defaults: `mt='xs'`, `mb='xxxs'`)
- **Example usage**: "20 de fevereiro" between transaction groups

#### Points Card Adapter
- Wraps `CardPoints` design component
- **Props**: `icon`, `title`, `valuePoints`, `iconInfo?`, `hideBottom?`, spacing
- **Default spacing**: `ml/mr='smaller'`, `mb='xxxs'`
- **Always sets**: `isLoading={false}`, `onPressIconInfo={() => {}}`

#### Voucher Card Adapter
- **High-complexity**: Maps extensive data to `CardVehicleTags` (version 2.0)
- **Tag mapping**: `{ success, error, alert, highlight }` → semantic colors
- **Props**: icon, vehicleName, title/titleDescription, date/dateNumber, statusTitle/status, typeTag, ghostButtonText
- **Always sets**: `type='extended'`, `version='2.0'`, stub button handler

---

## 3. Screen Renderer (`src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx`)

### Rendering Pipeline

```
Screen (JSON) → ScreenRenderer
  ├── isVisible(component, context) → evaluateRule() → boolean
  ├── separateConditionals(props) → { interpolatable, conditionals }
  ├── interpolateDeep(interpolatable, context) → resolved values
  └── getRenderer(type) → SDUIComponentRenderer
      └── <Renderer props={resolvedProps} context={context} />
```

### Key Steps

1. **Visibility Check**: `component.visible?.rule` evaluated against context
2. **Conditional Separation**: Props with `{ rule: ... }` are kept as-is (resolved by adapter or re-evaluated)
3. **Interpolation**: `{{context.path.to.value}}` in string/number props → actual values
4. **Registry Lookup**: Type → component renderer function
5. **Fallback**: Missing adapters render yellow warning with type/props

### Layout
```jsx
<View flex={1}>
  {header && renderComponent(header)}
  <View flex={1}>
    {body.map((component, idx) => renderComponent(component))}
  </View>
  {footer && renderComponent(footer)}
</View>
```

---

## 4. Mock Payloads

### Pattern: Mocks Structure
Located in `src/services/sdui/mocks/`:
- **File per screen**: `voucherList.ts`, `extractHistory.ts`, `voucherForm.ts`, etc.
- **Export variants**: `...Loaded`, `...Empty`, `...Filtered` for different states
- **Type**: `SDUIScreen`

### Example: Extract History (`extractHistory.ts`)

#### Context
```typescript
context: {
  balance: { pointsFormatted: '15.320' },
  transactions: [
    {
      icon: 'icon-tag',
      title: 'Pedágio Rodovia Anhanguera',
      value: '+ 150 pontos',
      valueColor: 'green',
      typeLabel: 'Ganhos',
    },
    // ... 7 more
  ],
}
```

#### Body Components (in order)
```
1. nav_header (title: 'Extrato')
2. points_card (balance display with interpolation)
3. section_heading (label: 'Lançamentos')
4. separator (horizontal line)
5. tab_bar (tabs: ['Todos', 'A expirar'])
6. date_separator (label: '20 de fevereiro')
7–8. transaction_item × 2 (interpolated from transactions[0–1])
9. date_separator (label: '18 de fevereiro')
10–11. transaction_item × 2 (interpolated from transactions[2–3])
... [pattern repeats for other dates]
```

#### Interpolation Examples
```json
{
  "type": "points_card",
  "props": {
    "valuePoints": "{{balance.pointsFormatted}}"  // Resolves to "15.320"
  }
},
{
  "type": "transaction_item",
  "props": {
    "icon": "{{transactions.0.icon}}",            // Resolves to "icon-tag"
    "value": "{{transactions.0.value}}",          // Resolves to "+ 150 pontos"
    "valueColor": "{{transactions.0.valueColor}}"  // Resolves to "green"
  }
}
```

#### Empty State
```typescript
extractHistoryEmpty: {
  context: { balance: { pointsFormatted: '500' } },
  body: [
    points_card, section_heading, separator, tab_bar,
    empty_state(icon: 'icon-invoice-error', title: 'Para ganhar pontos...')
  ]
}
```

---

## 5. Story Pattern (`src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx`, `SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`)

### Structure

```typescript
import ScreenRenderer from '../services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer';
import { registerAllAdapters } from '../services/sdui/adapters';
import { useSDUIJsonEditor } from '../../.storybook/addons/json-editor';
import { voucherListLoaded, voucherListEmpty } from '../services/sdui/mocks';

registerAllAdapters();  // One-time setup

export default function Demo() {
  const [view, setView] = useState<'list' | 'empty'>('list');
  const screen = useSDUIJsonEditor(SCREENS[view]);  // Edit JSON live
  
  return (
    <ScreenRenderer screen={screen} />
  );
}
```

### Two Story Patterns

1. **Simple Demo**: Single mode with toggle between states
   - ViewToggle component for 'list' | 'empty' | 'filtered'
   - JSON editor addon allows live editing of SDUI payload
   - Real-time rendering

2. **Side-by-Side Comparison**:
   - **Left**: Traditional React components hardcoded data
   - **Right**: Same visual via SDUI JSON + ScreenRenderer
   - Demonstrates that JSON-driven approach achieves identical UI
   - Shows decoupling benefit: backend can change content without deploy

### Extract Story Example
- `Demo`: Load/Empty toggle with JSON editor
- `Comparison`: Real screen (hardcoded) vs SDUI (JSON)
  - Real uses `CardPoints`, `Icon`, nested `Box`, manual date grouping
  - SDUI uses context data + adapters, backend controls layout

---

## 6. Existing Adapters Summary Table

| Adapter | Design Component | Props Style | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| HeadingAdapter | Text (headingMDL) | text, color, spacing | Simple mapping |
| TextAdapter | Text | text, type, color, spacing | Flexible typography |
| PointsCardAdapter | CardPoints | icon, title, valuePoints | Spacing defaults: ml/mr='smaller' |
| TabBarAdapter | Custom (Pressable + Text + Separator) | tabs, spacing | Internal state for active tab |
| TransactionItemAdapter | Custom (Box + Icon + Text) | icon, title, value, valueColor, typeLabel, showIcon | Color map: green→ACCEPT, red→DENY, etc. |
| DateSeparatorAdapter | Box + Text | label, spacing | Minimal: just a date label |
| VoucherCardAdapter | CardVehicleTags | icon, vehicleName, title, titleDescription, date, dateNumber, statusTitle, status, typeTag | Tag mapping; extensive props |
| BalanceDisplayAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| ButtonAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| LinkAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| AlertAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| ListTextIconAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| SeparatorAdapter | SeparatorHorizontal | bgColor, height, width, spacing | Horizontal line utility |
| IconTextRowAdapter | Box + Icon + Text | icon, iconColor, iconSize, text, textType, textColor, spacing | Flex row layout; reusable pattern |
| SectionHeadingAdapter | (inferred: Text) | text, spacing | Likely section labels |
| EmptyStateAdapter | Custom (Box + Icon + Box + Text) | icon, iconColor, bgColor, title, description | Centered card with rounded icon circle (80×80) |
| RichTextAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |
| PointsInputAdapter | (in codebase, not examined) | — | — |

---

## 7. Registry & Resolution (`src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts`)

### API
```typescript
// Create isolated registry
const registry = createRegistry();

// Register component
registerComponent(registry, 'my_type', MyAdapter);

// Retrieve for rendering
const Renderer = getRenderer(registry, 'my_type');

// Default registry (module singleton)
registerToDefault('heading', HeadingAdapter);
const defaultRegistry = getDefaultRegistry();
```

### Lookup in ScreenRenderer
```typescript
const Renderer = getRenderer(registry, component.type);
if (!Renderer) return <FallbackComponent type={...} props={...} />;
return <Renderer props={interpolatedProps} context={context} />;
```

---

## 8. Key Props/Spacing Values (Design System)

### Spacing Tokens
Common in SDUI props:
- `mt`, `mb`, `ml`, `mr`, `pd` (padding), `br` (border radius)
- Values: `'xxs'`, `'xs'`, `'xxxs'`, `'nano'`, `'smaller'`, `'small'`, `'medium'`, `'large'`, etc.

### Colors (Design System Palette)
- `INTENSE_COLOR_01` → `INTENSE_COLOR_05` (intensity scale)
- `ACCEPT_COLOR_01` (green)
- `DENY_COLOR_01` (red)
- `ALERT_COLOR_01` (orange)
- `BRAND_BASE_01`, `BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02`, `BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03`
- `LIGHT_BASE_01`, `LIGHT_BASE_02`

### Typography
- `type`: `'headingMDL'`, `'bodyMD'`, `'paragraphMD'`, `'paragraphXXXL'`, `'subtitleXXL'`, `'captionMD'`, `'captionSM'`, etc.

---

## 9. Mocks Export Pattern (`src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts`)

```typescript
export { voucherListLoaded, voucherListEmpty, voucherListFiltered } from './voucherList';
export { extractHistoryLoaded, extractHistoryEmpty } from './extractHistory';
export { voucherFormAbastecimento, voucherFormEstacionamento } from './voucherForm';
// ... etc
```

All mocks are `SDUIScreen` objects that can be rendered directly via `ScreenRenderer`.

5. Import in `adapters/index.ts` and register in `adapters` const

### To Use in Storybook:
1. Import `ScreenRenderer`, `registerAllAdapters()`, mock payload
2. Use `useSDUIJsonEditor(mockScreen)` hook for live JSON editing
3. Render: `<ScreenRenderer screen={screen} />`

# PR Review Report: SP_0005.1 — SDUI Demo: Extrato (Transaction History)

## Summary
Reviewed 7 files implementing SDUI support for transaction history screens. Implementation includes 4 new adapters, 2 mock payloads, 1 Storybook story, and 30 passing unit tests. The code demonstrates SDUI viability for list-based screens with tabs and grouped data.

---

## Issues Found

### BUG Issues

**BUG-1: TabBarAdapter has no functional tab switching** — **Severity: MAJOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 25-26
- **Issue**: The adapter maintains internal `activeTab` state via `useState(0)`, but this state change has no effect. The tabs don't filter transactions or trigger any callback. The tab UI changes but the content below remains static.
- **Impact**: Users can click tabs but nothing happens — broken UX pattern. The "A expirar" tab shows the same transactions as "Todos".
- **Production Risk**: If backend sends tab configurations expecting filtering behavior, users will experience a broken feature.
- **Recommendation**: Either:
  1. Remove tab functionality entirely until backend can provide filtered data per tab via context
  2. Add a `onTabChange` callback prop that fires an action/event
  3. Use context to drive tab content (e.g., `{{tabs.0.items}}`, `{{tabs.1.items}}`)

**BUG-2: Missing null safety in TransactionItemAdapter** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 60
- **Issue**: `String(icon)` coercion without validation. If `icon` is `undefined`, this becomes `"undefined"` string, which may not be a valid icon name and could cause runtime errors in the `Icon` component.
- **Impact**: Potential crash or broken icon rendering if backend sends malformed data.
- **Recommendation**: Add default value: `icon: String(icon || 'icon-help')`

**BUG-3: Hardcoded color value violates design system pattern** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 36
- **Issue**: Hardcoded `borderBottomColor: '#E91E63'` violates FACT24WR54SL09 (styles must come from design system)
- **Impact**: Color won't respect theme changes or design system updates. Inconsistent with other adapters that use design tokens.
- **Recommendation**: Use a design token color name or create a constant mapped to design system colors

---

### STYLE Issues

**STYLE-1: Inconsistent spacing prop types across adapters** — **Severity: NIT**
- **Files**: All 4 adapters
- **Issue**: PointsCardAdapter accepts `mt/mb/ml/mr` props, TabBarAdapter accepts `mt/mb/ml`, TransactionItemAdapter only `mb`, DateSeparatorAdapter `mt/mb`. Inconsistent API for similar components.
- **Impact**: Developer confusion when using these components. Why can't I set `mr` on DateSeparator?
- **Recommendation**: Standardize to either accept all spacing props or have a clear pattern (e.g., list items only accept `mb`, cards accept all 4)

**STYLE-2: Magic numbers for hardcoded spacing** — **Severity: NIT**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 29, 36
- **Issue**: Hardcoded values `12` and `16` for marginLeft and marginRight instead of design token strings
- **Impact**: Inconsistent with design system patterns, harder to maintain
- **Recommendation**: Map to design tokens (e.g., `ml === 'smaller' ? theme.spaces.smaller : 0`)

**STYLE-3: Unused context parameter** — **Severity: NIT**
- **Files**: All 4 adapters
- **Issue**: All adapters destructure `{ props, context }` but never use `context` parameter
- **Impact**: Misleading signature, suggests context is used when it's not
- **Recommendation**: Keep it for consistency with the adapter signature pattern (required by SDUIComponentRenderer type), but this is acceptable per the architecture

**STYLE-4: Missing JSDoc for adapter props interfaces** — **Severity: NIT**
- **Files**: All 4 adapters
- **Issue**: No documentation on prop interfaces (e.g., what does `valueColor: string` accept? What icon names are valid?)
- **Impact**: Backend developers sending JSON won't know valid values without reading implementation
- **Recommendation**: Add JSDoc comments with examples:
```typescript
interface TransactionItemProps extends SDUIComponentProps {
  /** Icon name from design system (e.g., 'icon-tag', 'icon-parking') */
  icon: string;
  /** Color key: 'green' | 'red' | 'gray' | 'orange' or design token */
  valueColor?: string;
}
```

---

### PERF Issues

**PERF-1: TabBarAdapter re-renders all tabs on every state change** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 30-46
- **Issue**: `.map()` runs on every render without memoization. Each tab is a new `Pressable` instance on every state update.
- **Impact**: Minor — only 2 tabs currently, but could degrade with many tabs
- **Recommendation**: Extract tab rendering to `React.memo` component or use `useMemo` for the tabs array

**PERF-2: Story component renders identical screen twice** — **Severity: NIT**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`
- **Lines**: 217-246
- **Issue**: `SideBySideComparison` renders both RealScreenPreview and SDUI version in the same component, both always mounted even when not visible
- **Impact**: Storybook only, negligible
- **Recommendation**: None needed for demo purposes

---

### MISSING Issues

**MISSING-1: No accessibility labels on tabs** — **Severity: MAJOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 33-45
- **Issue**: `Pressable` lacks `accessibilityRole="tab"`, `accessibilityState`, and `accessibilityLabel`
- **Impact**: Screen readers won't announce tabs correctly. Fails accessibility guidelines.
- **Recommendation**: Add:
```typescript
<Pressable
  accessibilityRole="tab"
  accessibilityState={{ selected: isActive }}
  accessibilityLabel={`${tab.title} tab`}
  // ...
/>
```

**MISSING-2: No test coverage for showIcon=false in mock payload** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts`
- **Lines**: 169
- **Issue**: Only one transaction (index 4, "Pontos expirados") uses `showIcon: false`. The unit test covers this, but the mock doesn't demonstrate the visual difference well.
- **Impact**: QA may miss testing this edge case
- **Recommendation**: Add another transaction with `showIcon: false` to make the pattern clearer

**MISSING-3: No empty tabs handling** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 24
- **Issue**: If `tabs` array is empty (despite default `= []`), renders nothing but still renders separator line
- **Impact**: Empty UI with just a line separator
- **Recommendation**: Add early return: `if (!tabs.length) return null;`

**MISSING-4: No error boundary for malformed transaction data** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx`
- **Issue**: If `title` or `value` are missing/null, component may render blank or crash
- **Impact**: Graceless degradation with bad backend data
- **Recommendation**: Add fallbacks: `title: String(title || 'Transaction')`, `value: String(value || '0')`

**MISSING-5: No visual indication that tabs are interactive** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`
- **Lines**: 33-45
- **Issue**: No hover/press visual feedback. Users may not realize tabs are clickable.
- **Impact**: Poor UX, especially on web
- **Recommendation**: Add `style` prop with `opacity` change on press or use design system Pressable wrapper

**MISSING-6: Story doesn't demonstrate tab switching** — **Severity: MAJOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`
- **Issue**: The story shows tabs but doesn't explain or demonstrate that they don't actually do anything (related to BUG-1)
- **Impact**: Misleading demo — stakeholders may think tab filtering works
- **Recommendation**: Add a note in the story description: "Note: Tab switching UI is implemented, but filtering logic requires backend support via context data."

**MISSING-7: No test for tab key fallback** — **Severity: NIT**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/adapters.test.tsx`
- **Lines**: 434-451
- **Issue**: Test doesn't verify the `key={tab.id ?? idx}` fallback when `id` is missing
- **Impact**: If a tab has no `id`, React may warn about duplicate keys
- **Recommendation**: Add test case with tabs that have no `id` field

**MISSING-8: No interpolation test for nested context** — **Severity: MINOR**
- **File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts`
- **Issue**: Mock uses `{{transactions.0.icon}}` pattern but no unit test verifies this deep array interpolation works correctly
- **Impact**: Interpolation logic is tested elsewhere, but not in the adapter tests
- **Recommendation**: Add integration test or verify in `interpolation.test.ts`

---

## Security Assessment

**SECURITY-1: No XSS risk** — **Status: PASS**
- All string values are rendered through React components, which escape by default
- No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` or raw HTML injection

**SECURITY-2: Icon name injection** — **Status: ACCEPTABLE**
- Icon names come from context interpolation (`{{transactions.0.icon}}`)
- Design system Icon component should validate icon names
- If invalid icon is passed, Icon component handles gracefully (per existing pattern)

**SECURITY-3: No prototype pollution** — **Status: PASS**
- Verified `interpolation.ts` has `DENIED_KEYS` protection against `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype` (lines 4, 16)

---

## Positive Findings

1. **Excellent test coverage**: 30 tests covering all adapters, including edge cases like `showIcon: false` and missing optional props
2. **Consistent with existing patterns**: Follows the same structure as VoucherCardAdapter and EmptyStateAdapter
3. **Good separation of concerns**: Adapters are pure view components, no business logic
4. **Type safety**: Proper TypeScript interfaces extending SDUIComponentProps
5. **Proper registration**: All 4 adapters registered in `index.ts` and `models/index.ts` SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES
6. **Good demo structure**: Story includes both demo and side-by-side comparison
7. **Context interpolation**: Properly uses `{{var}}` syntax for dynamic data
8. **Design system usage**: Correctly imports and uses DS components (Box, Text, Icon, CardPoints)

---

## Summary of Severity Counts

- **BLOCKER**: 0
- **MAJOR**: 3 (BUG-1, MISSING-1, MISSING-6)
- **MINOR**: 7 (BUG-2, BUG-3, PERF-1, MISSING-2, MISSING-3, MISSING-4, MISSING-5)
- **NIT**: 6 (STYLE-1, STYLE-2, STYLE-3, STYLE-4, MISSING-7, MISSING-8)

---

## Recommendations for Merge

### Must Fix Before Merge (Blockers)
- **None** — No blockers identified

### Should Fix Before Merge (Major)
1. **BUG-1**: Address non-functional tabs — either remove or document limitation
2. **MISSING-1**: Add accessibility labels to tabs
3. **MISSING-6**: Add documentation to story explaining tab limitation

### Can Fix in Follow-up PR (Minor/Nit)
- All STYLE and remaining MISSING issues
- BUG-2, BUG-3 (defensive coding improvements)
- PERF-1 (optimization)

---

## Final Verdict

**APPROVE with minor changes recommended**

The implementation is solid and demonstrates SDUI viability for list screens. All tests pass. The main concern is the non-functional tab switching (BUG-1 + MISSING-6), which should be documented to prevent confusion. The accessibility issue (MISSING-1) should be addressed before production use.

The code quality is consistent with existing SDUI adapters, follows project patterns (FACT247MM005), and properly uses the design system (FACT24WR54SL09). TypeScript errors are expected per FACT245KB31.

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts — evaluateRule() function with:
  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.
